commit.subject: Make Averguard quests more flexible

diff_evidence.commit_message:
Make Averguard quests more flexible

These changes remove the previous requirement of having to return to
Guill after finding the Key and the Talisman. Instead, players can
collect both items and head straight for Maddox. Then they only have to
make one trip back to Guill to get their rewards. Of course, they can
still return to Guill to turn those quests at any time in between.

Another change is the ability to grab both the Ukkonen and the Averguard
Tome quests at the same time.

commit.subject: Add "Ardwen" quest to Family Crypt

diff_evidence.commit_message:
Add "Ardwen" quest to Family Crypt

The teleport in Family Crypt is now a one-way-trip. The objective is to
escape from the crypt. This is done by finding Ardwen's ring, placing it
in a slot on the coffin, and defeating the zombie Ardwen. Killing Ardwen
will lift a curse on the lever that controls the exit door.

To ensure the player gets trapped after taking the teleporter, the game
is saved when they land on the opposite side of the center bridge.

Ardwen's combat numbers are experimental. I think I've been making boss
combat numbers too small, so I've tried giving Ardwen a sizable boost.

commit.subject: Adjust boss HP, damage, and cooldown; adjust acc/avoid/poise

diff_evidence.commit_message:
Adjust boss HP, damage, and cooldown; adjust acc/avoid/poise

The general formula for boss HP/damage/cooldown is as follows:

- HP is the HP of a matching normal enemy 2 levels higher, multiplied by
2. So for a level 3 boss zombie, we take the HP of a level 5 normal
zombie and multiply it by 2.
- Damage is equal to that of a normal enemy that is 2 levels higher.
- Cooldown is half that of a normal enemy

commit.subject: Refactor level 2-4 items

diff_evidence.commit_message:
Refactor level 2-4 items

Unchanged:
- level 2-4: one each of weapons, shield, and rings

Old items:
- level 2 & 3: two of each armor slot, a choice between two related stats
- level 4: both stats in one item

New items:
- level 2-4: three of each armor slot, one for each character class
- character titles used as prefixes for items (Brute Dagger, Scout
Shirt, Exiled Fire Ring, etc)

The reason I've made these changes is that it offers more variety in
equipment choice, especially for rare level 4 items. It also makes
dividing up later armors easier. For example, the mage robes can now
have a set with mage-related bonuses without disrupting the other groups
of armor.

commit.subject: Re-work Torture Chambers map/quest (again)

diff_evidence.commit_message:
Re-work Torture Chambers map/quest (again)

This is the beginning of an effort to remove quests that "trap" the
player. Such quests aren't effective when the player has teleport
scrolls.

There was also a problem with the layout of the spike traps. Placing the
traps at the junctions allowed the player to bypass them completely by
moving in between the tiles. The new layout fixes this by forcing the
player to hit the spike trap if they make a wrong turn.

The spike trap base damage has been reduced, as these traps now apply
bleeding damage.

The warden's notes themselves have been re-worked as well. Instead of
a map of each section, players get some story with a description of the
"rules" that will help them navigate the traps.

The warden's notes can be kept and placed in the player's private stash,
or they can be placed on the bookstand at the end of the trap section in
order to disable the traps.

diff_evidence.commit_message:
Empyrean: Fix floor gaps near wall tiles in grassland/cave maps

In the past, the wall tiles from these tilesets did not have the black
"mask" that covered the non-visible portion. This meant that we couldn't
draw floor tiles under them, as the floor would show on the back side.
However, this meant that there would be no floor on the *visible* side,
either.

But we have the masks now, so this commit fills in those gaps. The
automap rulesets have also been updated so that new maps will have the
floors filled correctly.

commit.subject: Iron Labyrinth Dungeon

diff_evidence.commit_message:
Iron Labyrinth Dungeon

This is a procedurally generated dungeon meant to showcase the new
features for version 1.15 (#973). It scales to the player's level and is
accessible after completing the Krolan quest.

The dungeon could definitely use more room variety. So that is something
I will continue to work on.

The Ruins tileset used here is by rubberduck. I have extended the
tileset with stairs, gates, pits (from the dungeon tileset), and an
assortment of objects and plants. Sources are in the flare-game-art-src
repo, which is linked under art_src/README.md

The dungeon boss also gets some unique art. I took some inspiration from
everyone's favorite Demon's Souls boss and slapped armor plating on
a big antlion. At the moment, the boss only has a basic antlion moveset.
I'll hopefully flesh this out a bit.

With the dungeon is the introduction of "Mastite" items. These are
powerful pieces of equipment with randomized stats. I'll probably add
these to the drop tables for boss chests in the main game.

commit.subject: Empyrean: Update collision tiles and better indicate spike traps

diff_evidence.commit_message:
Empyrean: Update collision tiles and better indicate spike traps

As per the previous rule file update, non-wall objects should now be
visible on the minimap. Before this commit, it was a mix where some maps
were showing these objects, and some weren't. It should be more
consistent now.

I've also take the opportunity while passing over the maps to use the
new bloody grate tile where spike traps exist. I had tried to leave some
other indications in the maps where spike traps were. But it wasn't
consistent in a way that a player could know there was a trap when
looking at it for the first time.

diff_evidence.commit_message:
Empyrean: Tweak MP regen per level/mental

The goal here is to make the Mental stat matter more than the player's
level. I was finding that, with no Mental investment, my MP was
regenerating at a rate that I didn't need Mana Potions on my melee
character.

  instances in a ring around Razu

  poison
- Warp Ball; slow-ish projectile that warps the player to one of eight
  random points in the boss arena. Has a long cooldown so that it's not
  too frustrating.
